CheckTitle: Conditional Access policy enforces sign-in frequency for non-corporate devices
Description:
Microsoft Entra **Conditional Access** policy with **sign-in frequency** controls how often users must re-authenticate when accessing resources from non-corporate devices.

This ensures that sessions on unmanaged devices are time-limited, reducing the window of opportunity for unauthorized access through stale sessions.
Risk:
Without sign-in frequency enforcement on non-corporate devices, user sessions may persist indefinitely on unmanaged devices.

- **Session hijacking** on shared or public devices becomes more likely
- **Stolen session tokens** remain valid for extended periods
- **Unauthorized access** from compromised personal devices

Code.Other:
1. Navigate to the Microsoft Entra admin center at https://entra.microsoft.com.
2. Expand **Protection** > **Conditional Access** and select **Policies**.
3. Click **New policy**.
4. Under **Users**, select **All users**.
5. Under **Target resources**, select **All cloud apps**.
6. Under **Conditions** > **Filter for devices**, configure a rule to target non-corporate devices (e.g., non-compliant and non-domain-joined).
7. Under **Session**, enable **Sign-in frequency** and set it to the desired interval (e.g., 1 hour).
8. Set the policy to **On** and click **Create**.

Recommendation.Text:
Configure a Conditional Access policy to enforce **sign-in frequency** on non-corporate devices. Use device filters to target unmanaged endpoints and set a time-based re-authentication interval to limit session duration.

This aligns with **zero trust** principles by ensuring sessions on untrusted devices are regularly validated.

Notes: This check corresponds to Maester test MT.1018 (Test-MtCaEnforceSignInFrequency). A qualifying policy must target all users, all applications, enforce time-based sign-in frequency, and include a device filter targeting non-corporate devices.
